title: An open letter & audio msg: PM Modi's multipronged appeal to Bengal voters
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi concluded his West Bengal election campaign with an open letter, expressing his vision for a 'Viksito Bangla' and highlighting the affection received. He emphasized the aspirations for change among youth, women, and all citizens, reaffirming his commitment to serving the state and assuring a BJP Chief Minister's swearing-in ceremony.

title: China to Mark Zuckerberg's Meta: You cannot buy this company
content: China has blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, citing national security concerns and potential technology leakage. This move follows reports of Beijing restricting American capital in its AI sector. The decision stems from Manus's "general AI agent" technology, developed in China but moved to Singapore, raising fears of intellectual property transfer to US firms.

title: Sanjay Dutt appears before NCW, apologises over ‘Sarke Chunar’ song
content: Actor Sanjay Dutt appeared before the National Commission for Women and apologized for the song 'Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke'. The song faced backlash for its suggestive lyrics and choreography. The commission had taken suo motu cognisance of the matter. The lyrical video has been removed from YouTube. Nora Fatehi also addressed the controversy earlier.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 40: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Pushpa 2’s all-time domestic record
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sara Arjun, is on the verge of becoming the second highest-grossing Indian film of all time globally, with a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,777.52 crore, just Rs. 10.54 crore away from surpassing 'Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's Rs. 1,788.06 crore record. On Day 40, the film's India net stands at Rs. 1,130.69 crore, approximately Rs. 103 crore away from 'Pushpa 2: The Rule's all-time domestic record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore.

title: Selfie tax? Sooryavanshi's 'paisa laaya' prank leaves kids confused - Watch
content: Rajasthan Royals shared the video on their social media platforms, showing the 15-year-old batter having some fun with two children who came up to him for a picture.

title: 'Why are you fighting? You're 80': SC issues notice in Sunjay Kapur estate dispute, urges meditation
content: The Supreme Court has issued a notice to Priya Sachdev Kapur and 22 others in an estate dispute involving late businessman Sanjay Kapur. The court suggested mediation to resolve the prolonged family litigation, emphasizing an amicable solution. Rani Kapur's plea seeks to restrain respondents from interfering with the estate and its assets, citing concerns over potential dissipation.

title: Apple's next CEO, John Ternus tells employees what Apple won't do with AI
content: Days after being named Apple's next CEO, John Ternus walked into a town hall and told employees what Apple won't do with AI: ship technology for technology's sake. With Siri still broken after two years of missed promises, Ternus laid out a two-part AI philosophy—one focused on internal data, the other on products that actually work. The question is whether Apple's restrained approach wins or falls further behind.

title: Fertiliser supply robust, no LPG dry out: Centre soothes energy supply concerns
content: Despite global energy supply strains due to Middle East conflict, India's government assures robust domestic fertilizer availability, exceeding current requirements. Commercial LPG allocations have also increased, with officials confirming sufficient supplies and no distributor dry-outs, even as panic buying is reported.

title: ‘Ek match mein open…’: Chahal’s hilarious plea to Preity Zinta leaves fans in splits
content: Punjab Kings are dominating IPL 2026, leading the table with six wins from seven matches. Their batting prowess, with Prabhsimran Singh and Shreyas Iyer in top form, is the driving force behind their success. Meanwhile, spinner Yuzvendra Chahal humorously requested an opening batting chance from co-owner Preity Zinta, despite a quiet season with the ball.

title: Grandfather's emotional words at Google campus leave Bengaluru techie teary-eyed
content: Bengaluru software engineer Manish Singh's journey from a humble background to Google is a testament to his family's unwavering belief in education. His mother's sacrifice and his grandparents' support paved the way for his success. Witnessing his grandfather's pride on the Google campus, a moment echoing childhood lessons, underscored the shared nature of his achievement.

title: Travis Kelce hints wedding date as Taylor Swift seeks privacy
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are all set to step into a new chapter in their lives after they have been together for close to three years as millions wait for their wedding. While Taylor Swift has maintained complete privacy, it seems the Kansas City Chiefs’ star player might have hinted at their wedding date.

title: Drop at your own peril: Teams pay heavy price for dropped catches
content: Dropped catches proved costly in IPL 2026, impacting multiple matches. KL Rahul and Shreyas Iyer capitalized on lifelines, leading to massive scores and altered game outcomes. Fielding errors by Punjab Kings and Rajasthan Royals, including dropped chances by Karun Nair and Aniket Verma, significantly influenced the results of high-scoring thrillers.

title: How Trump sanctions on China’s pvt refiner may have collateral damage beyond oil
content: Until now, the United States had largely focused its efforts on smaller Chinese companies and facilities in an attempt to curb Iran’s oil revenues, partly to avoid broader economic and diplomatic consequences.

title: 'Vaibhav Sooryavanshi probably using AI chip in bat': 15-year-old told to be sent to 'lab'
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is electrifying the cricket world with his fearless batting. Following a record-breaking 175 in the U19 World Cup final, he's now dominating IPL 2026, recently hitting a 37-ball century. His explosive performances have even led to humorous suggestions of him using an AI chip in his bat, highlighting his extraordinary talent.

title: Sunjay Kapur case: SC orders mediation in family dispute
content: The Supreme Court urged reconciliation in the Sona Group family trust dispute between Rani Kapur and Priya Sachdeva Kapur, highlighting the futility of prolonged litigation for an 80-year-old. Rani Kapur alleges fraudulent trust setup after a stroke, leading to her disinheritance. The court emphasized mediation for a peaceful resolution, noting related cases are pending.

title: Where is Dianna Russini? Sudden disappearance fuels speculation after Mike Vrabel scandal
content: Dianna Russini has gone silent following controversy linked to Mike Vrabel, sparking widespread speculation about her whereabouts. After viral Arizona photos and resurfaced past images, she denied any romantic connection, but the buzz persisted. She later resigned from The Athletic and deactivated her social media accounts. With no public appearances or updates since, questions continue to grow about her personal and professional future as the story remains unresolved.

title: Cricket's human machines: Meet Delhi Capitals’ unsung heroes
content: Throwdown specialists play a crucial role in preparing batters for high-speed deliveries and getting in the volume for skill-based sessions directed at fine tuning a particular shot. The Delhi Capitals employ Aniket Berde, Darshan, and Sai Pendam, each with unique backgrounds and skills. Their journeys reveal resilience, adaptation, and the evolving significance of throwdown experts in modern cricket.

title: AP SSC Result 2026: NOT before April 30, BSE AP to announce result date tomorrow after 8:30 pm
content: The Board of Secondary Education Andhra Pradesh (BSEAP) is expected to announce the AP SSC Result 2026 declaration date tomorrow after 8:30 pm, though the Class 10 results are not scheduled for release yet. Amid rumours of an April 25 result date, officials have denied false claims and urged caution. Over 6.40 lakh students appeared for exams held from March 16 to April 1. Results will be available online, with 35% pass criteria and supplementary.

title: TMC MP Mitali Bag’s car vandalised; party blames BJP, alleges 'attempt on her life'
content: Trinamool Congress alleged that BJP "goons" attacked Arambag MP Mitali Bag's car, vandalizing it and causing her injuries. The party accused Union Home Minister Amit Shah of ordering the attack, calling it an attempt on the MP's life. The incident occurred on the last day of campaigning for the second phase of West Bengal Assembly polls.

title: Virat Kohli on the brink of history, only 11 runs away from...
content: Virat Kohli is just 11 runs away from becoming the first player to reach 9000 IPL runs ahead of Delhi Capitals clash. With 328 runs this season, he continues his remarkable consistency. Dinesh Karthik praised Kohli’s ability to reinvent himself and his disciplined preparation, calling it a learning experience for younger players.

title: American Mom’s India Lessons: Kristen Fischer’s viral list reshapes daily life
content: In a charmingly candid Instagram post, Kristen Fischer, an American mother, shares the delightful lessons she's learned while living in India. Her viral insights redefine common terms like 'spicy,' 'five minutes,' and personal space, weaving an entertaining tapestry of cultural revelations.

title: Pregnancy changes everything: Why iron deficiency is not the only thing women need to fix
content: Pregnancy significantly alters a woman's body, demanding continuous adjustments to support new life. While iron and folic acid are crucial, a broader spectrum of micronutrients like B vitamins, calcium, vitamin D, magnesium, zinc, copper, and DHA are vital for maternal and fetal health. Addressing these nutritional needs holistically ensures healthier pregnancies and better long-term outcomes.

title: Gold price today: Yellow metal slips; check 24K, 22K city-wise rates
content: Gold prices saw a dip in futures trade on Monday, influenced by weaker global cues and subdued spot demand. While futures declined, retail prices across major Indian cities presented a mixed picture, with some experiencing slight increases and others remaining steady. International gold futures also edged lower, reflecting cautious global trading sentiment.

title: Engine on fire, exit jammed, botched evacuation: How the SWISS Delhi–Zurich flight horror unfolded
content: A Swiss International Air Lines flight aborted take-off at Delhi airport due to an engine fire, leading to a chaotic evacuation. A senior executive described the incident as "complete mismanagement" on the ground, citing issues with emergency response, passenger accounting, and hotel arrangements. The airline crew, however, was praised for their handling of the situation onboard.

title: West Bengal polls: Wafer-thin LS margin gives BJP heart in TMC lair
content: Rashbehari's election heats up as Trinamool's Debasish Kumar faces a strong challenge from BJP's Swapan Dasgupta. Despite Kumar's local roots and past wins, a narrow LS lead for Trinamool in the area signals a tight contest. Both candidates are actively campaigning, addressing issues from slum development to alleged corruption and extortion.

title: Brutal selloff: Infosys loses over Rs 2 lakh cr, slips out of top 10 most valued firms
content: Infosys faces investor pressure, with its market value dropping significantly despite strong March quarter results. A subdued FY27 revenue growth forecast and industry-wide shifts towards cost efficiency and AI-driven productivity are impacting its outlook. While AI presents new opportunities, it also pressures core business margins.

title: 'Abuse of cricket equipment': Angkrish Raghuvanshi fined by IPL
content: Angkrish Raghuvanshi was fined 20% of his match fee and given a demerit point for breaching the IPL Code of Conduct during Kolkata Knight Riders’ clash with Lucknow Super Giants. He reacted angrily after an obstructing-the-field dismissal. Despite the incident, KKR won a Super Over thriller, thanks to Rinku Singh’s unbeaten 83.

title: Maharashtra MSBSHSE HSC result this week amid protest concerns
content: Maharashtra State Board officials have assured that the MSBSHSE HSC results will be announced within the week, dismissing concerns about delays due to employee protests. Authorities confirmed that result preparation and verification are progressing on schedule. Anxiety had risen among students following media reports of disruptions, but the board reiterated there would be no impact. Results will be available online and via DigiLocker, with uploads already in the final stage of completion.

title: BLA claims 27 attacks in Balochistan in 10 days, says 42 Pakistani soldiers killed
content: The Baloch Liberation Army claims to have conducted 27 operations in Balochistan, resulting in 42 Pakistani military personnel killed and one captured.  Attacks included IED blasts, ambushes, and raids targeting security forces and pro-government individuals. The group also reported three of its fighters died. This follows a recent deadly attack on a Pakistan Coast Guard vessel.

title: India-New Zealand FTA signed: Duty on 100% Indian exports down to zero; top points to know
content: India-New Zealand FTA: It aims to double bilateral trade, provide Indian exporters duty-free access to the New Zealand market, and attract investments worth $20 billion over the next 15 years.

title: 'Watching Virat Kohli cry was emotional': Krunal Pandya opens up
content: After an 18-year wait, Royal Challengers Bengaluru clinched their maiden IPL title, defeating Punjab Kings by six runs. All-rounder Krunal Pandya expressed immense emotion, dedicating the historic victory to the passionate RCB fans and long-serving player Virat Kohli, who was visibly moved by the triumph.

title: No Indian yet in Esports Chess World Cup 2026: How can it change?
content: Magnus Carlsen won the Chess.com Open, while Denis Lazavik beat Nihal Sarin, leaving no Indian qualified yet for the Esports World Cup 2026, as of yet. Extra qualification spots don’t pass down, increasing pressure on Sarin. He remains India’s one of the besthopes, with chances through circuit rankings and qualifiers in the evolving esports chess format.

title: 'Good bye tukde-tukde INDI alliance': Kiren Rijiju on Raghav Chadha, 6 AAP MPs joining BJP
content: Union Minister Kiren Rijiju welcomed seven Rajya Sabha MPs who defected from AAP to BJP, praising their parliamentary conduct. The move significantly boosts BJP's strength in the Rajya Sabha, while AAP has petitioned for the disqualification of the defecting members.

title: DGCA grants licence to India’s first seaplane airline; service to start in Lakshadweep
content: India is set to relaunch seaplane services with SkyHop Aviation receiving its operator's license. The company will initially connect five Lakshadweep islands and the mainland using a 19-seater aircraft. This initiative, supported by government incentives and a modified De Havilland Canada DHC-6 Twin Otter, aims to improve regional connectivity and tourism.

title: One trick at a time, Krunal Pandya shows why skill, not match-ups, matters
content: Krunal Pandya has stood out for Royal Challengers Bengaluru in IPL 2026 by adapting brilliantly in a batter-dominated game. With clever variations, bouncers, and sharp tactics, he has handled all match situations. Despite higher economy, his fearless approach and innovation have impressed, with Dinesh Karthik calling him a “trend-setter” redefining finger-spin. Krunal now aims for an India comeback.

title: Parenting shift that makes kids say 'yes' more often; best time to use it
content: Expert unveils a simple parenting trick that empowers children by offering them two acceptable choices, both leading to the desired outcome. This technique fosters independence in kids and reduces daily battles over routines like mornings and bedtime. Parents can effectively use this to encourage cooperation and build confidence in their children.

title: Naidu sets ₹4.60-a-unit power purchase target for Andhra Pradesh by year-end
content: Naidu tell officials to bring down the State’s average power purchase cost to ₹4.60 a unit by the end of the year, and asked that more than half the State’s power generation come from renewable sources by 2028-29

title: 13 Nagapattinam fishermen assaulted mid-sea; one suspected Sri Lankan fisherman nabbed
content: They were robbed of their GPS gadgets and mobile phones and their catch. The Sri Lankan fisherman,  identified as Ayanthan of  Kankesanthurai, was captured by some Nagapattinam fishermen. He was handed over to the Department of Fisheries

title: U.S. Pacific Air Forces chief’s visit to India cements defence ties
content: While boosting Indo-Pacific defence ties, a major focus of the visit was advancing logistics cooperation, a statement by the American Embassy says

title: Meeting presses for social and economic security of domestic workers
content: The union submits a petition to the Labour Inspector of Mysuru seeking fulfillment of their demands including the immediate setting up of a Welfare Board for Domestic Workers

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: Poll campaign for phase-2 wraps up in Bengal amid charges, counter-charges by BJP, TMC
content: BJP accuses TMC of turning West Bengal into a 'haven for infiltrators', while Mamata Banerjee alleged that the saffron party was indulging in divisive politics

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026 | Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan, Ajith Kumar and others cast their votes
content: Other stars from Tamil Nadu’s film fraternity, like Vikram, Sivakarthikeyan and Sasikumar, were also seen voting at their respective polling booths in the state

title: YSRCP falsely claiming credit for Google Data Centre in Vizag: TDP MLC
content: Anuradha says groundwork laid under N. Chandrababu Naidu from 2014; alleges attempts to stall project earlier, sees 1.80 lakh jobs potential

title: High-octane poll campaign for phase-2 wraps up in Bengal amid charges, counter-charges by BJP, TMC
content: Several BJP leaders, including Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah campaigned in the State, attacking the TMC over alleged deterioration in law and order, rising crimes against women, political unrest, infiltration and corruption

title: Court stays proceedings in Maha Kumbh Mela ‘viral’ girl’s age dispute case
content: Private complaint alleges that she got married while still a minor, and sought to arraign the political leaders who attended her wedding at a temple in Thiruvananthapuram on March 11

title: Will not appear before Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma in excise policy case: Kejriwal
content: AAP supremo says that everyone knows that he was trapped in a ‘false’ case and sent to jail as the elected government in Delhi was ‘wrongfully brought down’

title: Consumer commission orders insurer to honour accident claim, pay compensation
content: Kottayam District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission observes that insurance company failed to raise any genuine doubt regarding authenticity of accident or establish any violation of policy conditions

title: Safeguard utilities before commencing roadworks, warns DC; officials to be held accountable if roads dug up after completing work
content: This comes in the light of the approval granted for white-topping works on 12 roads in the city with the first phase beginning on Manandavadi Road, Bogadi Road, and Vishwamanava Double Road

title: Officials told to work in a coordinated manner to benefit SCs, STs
content: The Karnataka State Commission for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes chairman convenes meeting at Suvarna Soudha in Belagavi

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Over 84% voter turnout in the State, highest in its electoral history
content: Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar said that it was the highest percentage of polling in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu. “[The] ECI salutes each voter of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu,” he said

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Policeman stabbed at polling booth in Poompuhar constituency; ex-serviceman arrested
content: Preliminary inquiry revealed that the suspect allegedly harboured resentment towards police personnel and had been involved in a similar assault on a policeman a few years ago

title: AAP's mask is off, gave Rajya Sabha seats for money: Congress
content: Senior Congress leader Ajay Maken alleged that the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) has been giving Rajya Sabha party tickets to the rich and wealthy in exchange for money and should thus change its name to the "Arabpati Aadmiyon ki Party [party of billionaires]"

title: MP Assembly: Congress stages walkout over Speaker's refusal to accept private bill on women's quota
content: The Opposition members walked out of the House after Speaker Narendra Singh Tomar declined to take up the bill, ruling that a government resolution on the issue — scheduled to be moved by Chief Minister Mohan Yadav — had already been listed

title: Victims of Chhattisgarh power plant explosion received compensation, says Vedanta
content: The company said it has provided financial assistance of ₹35 lakh each to the families of the deceased, while ₹15 lakh has been disbursed to those injured; had handed entire responsibility of the plant to NSGL, writes Vedanta Group chairman Anil Agrawal

title: 2030 Commonwealth Games: Supreme Court orders status quo on Asaram's ashram land
content: The Sant Shri Asharam Trust, which manages the ashram, has challenged in the apex court the April 17 order of the Gujarat High Court rejecting its appeals against the takeover of land on the grounds of violation of lease conditions and encroachment

title: ‘Not all seven MPs can be wrong’: Raghav Chadha on exit from AAP
content: Raghav Chadha said the work environment in Arvind Kejriwal’s party had turned ‘toxic’ with leaders stopped from working and asserted that ‘one or two persons can be wrong, but not all seven’

title: Tiger cub taken into captivity in the Nilgiris
content: Officials said local residents had noticed the tiger cub and informed the Forest department about the presence of two cubs in Solada village

title: Swatantryada Ota: The production wins hearts in Shivamogga
content: Swatantryada Ota, the play, has been produced by Shivamogga Rangayana with the financial support of the State Government in its budget.

title: India, New Zealand ink “historic” free trade agreement, to be implemented later this year
content: The FTA, negotiations for which were announced in March 2025 and concluded in December 2025, is one of the fastest to have been negotiated by India

title: Andhra Pradesh fuel crisis: Naidu orders three-hourly reports; officials deny shortage
content: District authorities denied any shortage and urged the public not to panic-buy, even as queues built up across Visakhapatnam, Anantapur and Rayalaseema

title: PM Modi leads mega roadshow in Sikkim
content: On Tuesday (April 28, 2026) the PM will participate in the closing ceremony of Sikkim's 50th statehood celebrations at Paljor Stadium

title: TDP condemns disruption of comedian Sarat Uday’s Bengaluru comedy show, calls it undemocratic
content: State president Palla Srinivasa Rao said any objections to expression must be addressed through legal channels, not through confrontational action by party workers, after a stand-up performance by Sarat Uday in Bengaluru was disrupted on Saturday

title: Tamil Nadu police track returnees from Southeast Asia in cyber slavery crackdown
content: The objective is to gather intelligence about victims detained in scam compounds in Laos, Cambodia, and Myanmar

title: Ladakh L-G Saxena approves creation of 5 new districts
content: With the creation of five new districts — Nubra, Sham, Changthang, Zanskar and Drass — Ladakh will now have seven districts

title: Biryani, Watermelon, Then 4 Deaths: A Tragedy That Played Out Over 12 Hours
content: Food poisoning is being suspected behind the suspicious deaths of the four family members within a gap of 12 hours.

title: Hindu Kush Snow Deficit Raises Water Security Fears For 2 Billion People
content: A fresh report from the International Centre for Integrated Mountain Development (ICIMOD) in Kathmandu shows that seasonal snow cover in the HKH has hit its lowest level in more than 20 years.

title: Sexual Matters Must Not Determine "Unity Or Division Of Church": Pope Leo
content: Priests and bishops in the global Church have long emphasised as high priorities its teachings on sexual issues

title: Hormuz Traffic Remains Dry Amid Chokehold, 7 Ships Cross Strait In 24 Hours
content: The vessels included ships leaving from Iraqi ports and one dry bulk vessel from an Iranian port, according to ship tracking data from Kpler.

title: Jobless Surat Man Kills Dietitian Wife, Buries Her. A Note Gives Him Away
content: After Vishal went missing, his minor son found a "chit" and handed it over to the police. The note contained a confession, stating that he "committed a crime" and his wife's body was in his "ancestral home at Salabatpura".

title: Epstein Trial In New Mexico Pushes Survivors To Reach Out To Authorities
content: Authorities in New Mexico are trying to determine how many local women and girls were abused by Jeffrey Epstein at his Zorro Ranch compound.

title: 88 Tiger Deaths Unresolved Since 2020, Multiple Unexplained, Reveals RTI
content: Right to Information (RTI) documents have revealed that 88 tiger deaths recorded between 2020 and 2021 remain unresolved, with no confirmed cause, no completed investigation, and no accountability fixed.

title: Screams, Cracked Windshield: Trinamool MP's Facebook Live Amid "Attack"
content: The footage shot from the rear seat shows the vehicle moving along a road when a large group of men suddenly surrounds it

title: No Supreme Court Relief For Gurugram Builder Floors Amid Demolition Drive
content: The demolition drive is part of an anti-encroachment exercise planned by the Haryana Shahari Vikas Pradhikaran (HSVP), which is set to cover 44 sectors of Gurugram starting today

title: 'Glorious Leader?' OpenAI Co-Founder's Diary To Play Key Role In Musk Trial
content: Greg Brockman's diary entry is part of the thousands of pages of documents revealed in court since Elon Musk sued OpenAI in 2024.

title: Hyderabad Woman's Morning Run Shocker, Catches Man In Obscene Act
content: According to the woman, the incident took place around 6:15 AM while the track was already busy with joggers and cyclists.

title: Woman Goes Into Labour On Train To Bengaluru, Passengers Help Deliver Baby
content: The woman, identified as Ponnamma, was travelling towards Mysuru when she began experiencing severe labour pain.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Trump's 'Hellhole' Comment Might Just Be A Favour To India
content: Trump is a symptom, not the system. He has just removed the euphemism, the 'polite' version of the condescension the Empire had so far hid behind. If anything, the question now is what to do with this clarity.

title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Offers To Open Strait Of Hormuz, Gives US Conditions
content: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif during a phone call Saturday night that the US "should first remove operational obstacles, including the blockade"

title: Trump To Hold Talks On Iran War With Top Security Advisors Today: Report
content: Tehran's top diplomat blamed Washington on Monday for the failure of talks in Pakistan this month -- the first and only round of negotiations in a bid to strike a deal to end the conflict.

title: White House Dinner Shooter Cole Allen To Appear In Court Today
content: The suspect is scheduled to make his first appearance in Washington federal court, two days after authorities said they foiled the attack.

title: Delhi Sizzles With Temperature Over 40 Degrees. Will It Rain Tomorrow?
content: The maximum temperature may reach 44 degrees today in Delhi and NCR, but relief might just be on its way.

title: "Historic Decision": Ladakh Adds 5 New Districts To Boost Remote Governance
content: For residents of far-flung villages tucked in the Himalayas, this promises something tangible: offices closer home, faster paperwork, and services that no longer require days of travel.
